Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees After Order To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF U S GLASS & ALUMINUM INC OF NEVADA's Motion For Attorney's Fees After Order To Compel Arbitration.

Plaintiff's "motion for attorney's fees after order to compel arbitration" as a Civil Code 1717 prevailing party is denied.

In this construction defect action, plaintiff won a motion to compel arbitration in San Francisco of its dispute with Winco Window Co. Winco's simultaneous motion to compel arbitration in Missouri was declared moot. Plaintiff now seeks attorney fees for the two motions.

The opinion plaintiff's memorandum primarily relies on - Otay River Constructors v. San Diego Expressway (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 796 - is inapposite. There, the action was "brought solely to compel arbitration." (Id. at 799.) Here, in contrast, the construction defect action is not solely to compel arbitration and was filed seven weeks before the first motion to compel. Even in the compel-solely scenario, the better view is that there can be only one Civil Code 1717 prevailing party in given litigation, so an attorney fee award at an early stage is improper. (Frog Creek Partners, LLC v. Vance Brown (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 515, 538-39, 543.) A contrary rule would foment needless litigation with serial fee motions.

The other opinion plaintiff relies on - Benjamin, Weill & Mazur v. Kors (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 40 - is also factually distinguishable. There, unlike here, the case had already been through motions in the trial court to confirm and vacate the arbitral award before the appeal was decided. (Id. at 50.) To the extent Kors conflicts with Frog Creek, the latter is better reasoned. (See 206 Cal.App.4th at 536-37.)

Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. , Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
